steps.title: Separate the two sentences
steps.content: The first clause says the artists were based in Venice in the sixteenth century. The next clause says this group is called the Venetian School. Those are complete ideas, so they need a full sentence boundary.

steps.title: Place fittingly with the name
steps.content: A and C create comma splices between independent clauses. B separates the clauses but attaches fittingly to the first sentence, where it does not logically belong. The period plus introductory fittingly correctly frames the naming of the group. The answer is D.
